channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: It’s too big and needs to be pruned to make it safe again, 28th March
item.description:
The tree needs regular pruning to keep it safe

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Appledore Crescent, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken pavement bollard, 28th March
item.description:
Bollard has been sheered off exposing a hole in the footway. Also the it appeared to be heavy plastic so there is a jagged edge protruding from the hole.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mill Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 3 items dumped, 28th March
item.description:
I have reported this recently and one item was taken but 3 other lots were left. They are in the alleyway between 40 and 38. There is a big red bag further down the alley and a large bag of rubbish behind the lamp post.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cantwell Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped rubbish on grass verge, 28th March
item.description:
Yet again more dumped rubbish probably the same moron as in recent weeks. I was cleaning up this morning as a ‘street friend’ - Copers Cope Road and into lower Worsley Bridge Road, but there is yet another large bag of rubbish dumped on the grass verge blowing its contents everywhere in the wind. It is very disheartening trying to keep this area clean as the local sports grounds do nothing to help. Please contact them to assist in cleaning up after the sports events. Furthermore put up CCTV cameras to prevent this happening in the future as a deterrent.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Worsley Bridge Road, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 28th March
item.description:
Multiple graffiti on buildings and windows

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Trafalgar Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Increase council tax on dog owners, 28th March
item.description:
Dogs shit and leaving dog poo bags on pavement is health hazard and requires additional street cleaning from a council that currently can't afford it's local responsibility Please increase council tax on dog owners so streets and parks. Can be kept clean from these individual as threat of fine is working if you never fine

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 60, Bridge Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Electric goods and general litter dumping, 28th March
item.description:
Perhaps this area would benefit from CCTV. A couple of HMO s nearby might be contributing to recurring litter problems in locality.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Widecombe Road, Mottingham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 lime bikes abandoned in tower block stairwell, 28th March
item.description:
2 lime bikes abandoned in Kingsgate estate ground floor tower block stairwell. One been there since Wednesday, second one joined it Friday

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tottenham Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Glass smashed, 28th March
item.description:
Glass from bus stop smashes all over pathway

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dunkery Road, Mottingham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Damage - smashed glass or panels / jagged metal edges on shelter

item.title: Bus shelter 0104 0183 at bus stop P Mayplace Rd West / Bexleyheath Clock Twr towards Crayford : bus shelter and Countdown Board no power - in darkness, 28th March
item.description:
Bus shelter 0104 0183 at bus stop P Mayplace Rd West / Bexleyheath Clock Twr towards Crayford : bus shelter and Countdown Board no power - in darkness See photographs
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Market Place, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: By salvation army church, 28th March
item.description:
Broken glass on the road needs to be cleaned. Also all down Chichester road is a mess

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chichester Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Excessive amount of bikes locked, 28th March
item.description:
There are several bikes locked, making it impossible for a wheel chair to pass. They are all locked with the same lock so someone is using the pavement as their personal storage space, to the detriment of road users, especially those with disabilities.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Beehive Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping and rubbish outside 5-9a sewdley st, e5 0ax, 28th March
item.description:
Flytipping and rubbish outside the flats (5-9A Sewdley St, E5 0AX)
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sewdley Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Plastic, 28th March
item.description:
Dumped

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Homerton Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tip ceres road, 28th March
item.description:
Fly tipping needs cleaning

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): St Nicholas Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole outside No.1-3 Vambery Road, 28th March
item.description:
This pothole has been here for 2 months and reported 3 times to the council. It now damaging both vehicle and tyres. Repair costs to vehicles will be claimed from the council.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Vambery Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: J4E UD black mercedes parked on double yellow near to the junction of Manton Road and Thorneycroft drive, 28th March
item.description:
J4E UD black mercedes parked on double yellow lines close to the junction of Manton Road and Thorneycroft road in Enfield Island Village on Saturday 28/3/26. Is it possible for these double yellow lines to be repainted as they are quite faded on this section. There are repeated parking of vehicles in this section of Manton Road in EIV and it's dangerous. Thank you.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Manton Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sign fallen down to eye height, 28th March
item.description:
This sign has slipped down the pole and is now at head height, and the sharp corner at eye level. Saw someone walk into it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Effingham Road, Hither Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped washing machine, 28th March
item.description:
Been there for a few days.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Victoria Rise, Clapham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken glass and Litter Maple Crescent side of Candy Box, 28th March
item.description:
This is a further request following a previous report submitted to Fix my Street with no action being taken on the matter. Unfortunately more litter has accumulated as a result. Please can this area be given a thorough clean to remove the broken glass which is a danger to animals and pedestrians together with other litter from the footfall from the shopping parade. Could I suggest that an overlap/attention be made to clean this area regularly when undertaking street cleaning of the area on Blackfen Parade shop frontage. Thank you I will be monitoring this situation

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Maple Crescent, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
